definition: Autonomous emergence of collusive pricing behavior among algorithmic agents through reinforcement learning without explicit communication.
etymology: Portmanteau combining "algorithm" + "collusion". The construction elegantly preserves the morphological clarity of both root concepts while creating a memorable 4-syllable term. The blended form follows successful economics neologism patterns (cf. "tokenomics" = token + economics). The double-L at the junction maintains pronounceability while signaling the collusion component.
